comment: Flywheel seed corpus — hand-curated failure→fix instances for training the deterministic layers. Each instance simulates a 'broken' file and the known-good 'fixed' version. The harness replays each through the AST + rule layers; if a layer produces the fix, that's a flywheel hit (free). If all miss, it would fall through to Claude (paid). The Claude-call ratio over time is the moat metric — see scripts/flywheel-stats.js.

instances.note: Classic MITM-vulnerable pattern — flag flipped from staging.
XSS → session takeover unless httpOnly is true.
Logging req.body leaks passwords / tokens — GDPR / PCI-DSS violation.
parseFloat on money — drift accumulates. Switch to decimal library.
Stale feature flag — collapse the dead branch.
Empty catch hides bugs. At minimum log the error.
forEach doesn't await — fire-and-forget bug. Use Promise.all + map.

instances.broken:
name: nightly
on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 0 31 2 *'
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo ok
instances.fixed:
name: nightly
on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 0 1 * *'
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo ok
instances.note: Feb 31 never exists — job will silently never fire.
Same MITM vector as rejectUnauthorized but on the request/got family.
Global nuclear TLS disable — kill the line entirely.
